DECLARATION OF GREG W. CHAMBERS IN SUPPORT OF REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO VERIFIED PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE AND OR PROHIBITION CLAIM FOR DAMAGES AND ATTORNEYS FEES (TRANSACTION ID # 58411665) FILED BY RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA DEMUR TO PETITIONER WILLIAM VEDERMAN'S VERIFIED PETITION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE AND/OR PROHIBITION, ETC. PETITIONER FILED NO OPPOSITION. RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CHALLENGES SET FORTH IN THE PETITION ARE TIME-BARRED AND THE PETITION DOES NOT STATE A COGNIZABLE CLAIM. PETITIONER HAS MADE NO EFFORT TO EXPLAIN HOW HE MIGHT AMEND AND, ON THIS RECORD, IT IS NOT EVIDENT THAT PETITIONER COULD AMEND. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Mar-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Deputy Attorney General GREG W. CHAMBERS of the Office of the Attorney General of the State of California, Tel No. 415-703-5723 appearing for Respondent Medical Board of California;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter heard at 2:00 p.m. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Respondent Medical Board of California demur to Petitioner William Vederman's Verified Petition for Peremptory Writ of Mandate and/or Prohibition, etc. Petitioner filed no opposition. Respondent Medical Board's request for judicial notice is GRANTED. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The challenges set forth in the petition are time-barred and the petition does not state a cognizable claim. Petitioner has made no effort to explain how he might amend and, on this record, it is not evident that Petitioner could amend. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
